title: Does Shinji actually know that there is certain number of
display_title: Does Shinji actually know that there is certain number of
wikitext:
[]: Does Shinji actually know that there is certain number of             Quote Post by greatmightypoo » Wed Oct 06, 2010 3:02 pm Angels to beat, till he can return to a normal life? Or does he actually think that he has to be a pilot his whole life, from now on? Cause if that's the case, his attidute towards piloting the Eva would be a lot more undertandable.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Wed Oct 06, 2010 3:11 pm He thinks he has to pilot his whole life I'm pretty sure, or until they kill all the angels at the very least. Only Gendo, Fuyu, Ritsuko, SEELE and maybe Kaji know anything about the actual number angels I believe.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 06, 2010 3:16 pm He probably hasn't really thought about it, too busy dealing with (or avoiding) the present and longing for the past to even consider the existence of the future.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 06, 2010 3:17 pm Pretty much everyone was goofing off like it was end of term in EoE -- but somehow he didn't get the memo.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Oct 06, 2010 6:08 pm You could make a whole show solely out of the things in Eva that Shinji doesn't know. I always find it amusing how Asuka, who has absolutely zero relevance to the overarching plot besides the effect she has on Shinji, knows more about it than he does (and there are a couple of things she knows that seem a little strange for her to know - are we intended to assume that she was aware of the existence of the Eva Series? When she mentions them in 25', she doesn't ask, "they're real?" but rather, "they were completed?"). Shinji forgot half the plot of the show before it even began. You don't get much more ignorant than that.

[]:             Quote Post by Stuart » Wed Oct 06, 2010 9:58 pm In that respect Shinji represents the audience: "What the fuck is going on here?"

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 07, 2010 2:14 pm Azathoth wrote: Asuka, who has absolutely zero relevance to the overarching plot besides the effect she has on Shinji, knows more about it than he does (and there are a couple of things she knows that seem a little strange for her to know - are we intended to assume that she was aware of the existence of the Eva Series? When she mentions them in 25', she doesn't ask, "they're real?" but rather, "they were completed?").

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Oct 07, 2010 2:35 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: But, given how Eva piloting is so important to her

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 07, 2010 3:24 pm Well, yeah. I thought Asuka (and speical snowflake Rei) growing up with the Eva program didn't need to be mentioned because it went without saying.

[]:             Quote Post by Assasinate » Mon Oct 11, 2010 2:51 am apparently... it's quite imposible to beat an ENEMY that knows him physically and mentally

[]:             Quote Post by ebm93 » Wed Oct 13, 2010 11:36 am Maybe Shinji just didn't want to know. He just doesn't enjoy piloting, so the less he hears about work the best, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Oct 13, 2010 1:14 pm ebm93 wrote: Maybe Shinji just didn't want to know. He just doesn't enjoy piloting, so the less he hears about work the best, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Assasinate » Sat Oct 16, 2010 7:29 am Sachi wrote: Shinji has on multiple occassion shown interest in the kind of things that happen in Nerv. In Episode 4 he asks Misato and Ritsuko about his father's work, again in Episode 17 to Kaji. In Episode 11 he asks Asuka why they're fighting Angels, and really begins pondering about the war and why it's being fought. It's probably safe to say Shinji does have an interest in this sort of thing, he's just kept out of the loop.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Oct 16, 2010 8:44 am He did ask/think about NERV-things, so I think that he has probably thought about it, but didn't really dare to ask because he's too shy or because he's afraid that he could have to keep fighting forever and prefers to stay unaware. I don't think any of the EVA-Pilots would be capable of giving a real answer if you asked them what they want to do in the future...

[]:             Quote Post by lilim » Thu Nov 25, 2010 12:17 am Sachi wrote: Shinji has on multiple occassion shown interest in the kind of things that happen in Nerv. In Episode 4 he asks Misato and Ritsuko about his father's work, again in Episode 17 to Kaji.
